questions.question: Why does ABR streaming use HTTP instead of RTSP?
options: HTTP segments are cacheable by existing CDNs and do not require a special streaming server
questions.explanation: ABR breaks video into HTTP-fetchable segments that any CDN cache can serve, reusing the existing HTTP infrastructure without needing stateful streaming servers.

questions.question: What triggers a quality downgrade in an ABR client?
options: Buffer level falling below the low watermark or bandwidth dropping below the current bitrate
questions.explanation: The ABR client monitors buffer level and bandwidth. If buffer drops below the low watermark or bandwidth cannot sustain the current quality, it downgrades to avoid rebuffering.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the safety margin in ABR quality selection?
options: It prevents selecting a bitrate too close to the estimated bandwidth, leaving headroom for fluctuations
It reduces the number of segments
questions.explanation: The safety margin (e.g., 0.7) means the client only uses 70% of the estimated bandwidth for quality selection, avoiding rebuffering when bandwidth dips slightly.

questions.question: What is the difference between HLS and DASH manifest formats?
options: HLS uses M3U8 text playlists; DASH uses MPD XML files
HLS uses UDP; DASH uses TCP
HLS is for audio only; DASH is for video
questions.explanation: HLS (Apple) uses M3U8 text playlist files, while DASH (MPEG) uses MPD XML manifests. Both serve the same purpose but with different syntax and features.
